message:
feat(cap): browser entropy, time, and host API capability subsystems with explicit authority boundaries

Extend the IoCap framework with three new browser-oriented capability
subsystems, each following the established default-deny posture with
explicit authority grants:

Browser Entropy (BrowserEntropyIoCap):
- BrowserEntropySource enum (WebCryptoGetRandomValues, DeterministicSeeded)
- BrowserEntropyAuthority with per-source grants and max-bytes-per-request
- BrowserEntropySupport matrix for runtime capability detection
- BrowserEntropyPolicyError with InvalidByteCount, SourceDenied,
  UnsupportedSource, RequestTooLarge variants

Browser Time (BrowserTimeIoCap):
- BrowserTimeSource enum (PerformanceNow, DateNow, DeterministicVirtual)
- BrowserTimeAuthority with per-source grants and monotonic enforcement
- BrowserTimeSupport matrix (NONE, STANDARD_BROWSER, FULL)
- BrowserTimePolicyError with MonotonicRequirementDenied variant

Browser Host API (BrowserHostApiIoCap):
- BrowserHostApi enum (ClipboardRead/Write, GeolocationRead, Notifications,
  BroadcastChannel, WebLocks, CacheStorage)
- BrowserHostApiAuthority with user-gesture requirement for sensitive APIs
- BrowserHostApiSupport matrix for runtime host API detection
- BrowserHostApiPolicyError with MissingUserGesture variant

Additionally introduces three generic (non-browser-prefixed) capability
abstractions for cross-platform use:
- EntropyIoCap/EntropyAuthority/EntropyRequest with source, operation, and
  byte-length policy enforcement
- TimeIoCap/TimeAuthority/TimeRequest with source, operation, and duration
  floor/ceiling enforcement
- HostApiIoCap/HostApiAuthority/HostApiRequest with surface-level grants and
  degraded-mode denial

Security invariant tests validate:
- Default-deny posture for all three subsystems
- Oversized entropy byte requests rejected
- Non-monotonic time source denial (DateNow denied when only PerformanceNow
  granted)
- Duration floor enforcement (1ms sleep rejected under 5ms minimum)
- Host API degraded mode denied without explicit grant
- Explicit grants pass correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
